name: Astrud Gilberto
abstract: Astrud Gilberto (Portuguese: [asˈtɾud ʒiwˈbɛʁtu]; born Astrud Evangelina Weinert; March 29, 1940 – June 5, 2023) was a Brazilian and American bossa nova and samba singer. She was the first wife of Brazilian bossa nova guitarist João Gilberto, whose surname she continued to use professionally after their divorce in 1964.

concerts.label: Stan Getz and his Quartet with Special Guest Star João Gilberto

works.t: Carnival in Flanders: Here's That Rainy Day
State Fair: It Might as Well Be Spring
Garota de Ipanema (The Girl from Ipanema)
Tonight I Shall Sleep (With a Smile on My Face)

about_quotes.speaker: Tony Marino
about_quotes.evidence: I started with string bass ‘cause of my sister’s love for a Stan Getz record which had Astrud Gilberto on it, “The Girl from Ipanema.”
about_quotes.doc_title: Tony Marino interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, August 25, 2025

about_quotes.speaker: Tom Lellis
about_quotes.evidence: in the sixties I was aware of Stan Getz and Astrud Gilberto and Joao Gilberto
about_quotes.doc_title: Tom Lellis interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clinton, New York, July 31, 2007

about_quotes.speaker: Gary Burton
about_quotes.evidence: Astrud Gilberto figures into this story a little bit. Stan and Astrud had a huge falling-out. She toured with the band in 1964... Stan meanwhile was paying her like a token $150 a week or something to tour with us.
about_quotes.doc_title: Gary Burton — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Toots Thielemans
about_quotes.evidence: [INTERVIEWER] Now, later in the ‘90s, you did two albums—the Brasil Project Volume I and II... [INTERVIEWER] But this starts before with Astrud Gilberto… in 1967
about_quotes.doc_title: Toots Thielemans — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Benny Carter
about_quotes.evidence: [INTERVIEWER] Was Astrud Gilberto in that? [INTERVIEWEE] In a nightclub scene, with the Stan Getz quartet.

about_quotes.speaker: Bobby Militello
about_quotes.evidence: the first album that I really memorized everything on was Stan Getz, Astrud Gilberto and “Desifinado”
about_quotes.doc_title: Bobby Militello interviewed by Monk Rowe, Buffalo, New York, October 14, 2002
